covla.label: CoVLA
covla.data_description: The CoVLA dataset was not used in its original form in this study; instead, a subset was selectively curated through a traffic-congestion handling process. To focus on driving scenes acquired on motor vehicle-only roads, we extracted 415 motorway videos from the original CoVLA dataset. From these selected motorway videos, a total of 672 clips were obtained. To ensure the diversity of driving maneuvers represented in the dataset, these clips were categorized into 445 lane-keeping (LK) clips and 227 lane-changing (LC) clips. The extracted clips exhibit a duration distribution ranging from approximately 10 to 30 seconds, thereby covering a wide range of real-world motorway driving situations. This curated subset of the CoVLA dataset was used for the subsequent experiments.

dashcam.label: dashcam
dashcam.data_description: The dataset used in this study was collected from a motor vehicle-only expressway located near Cheonan-Asan, South Korea (about 7~km). Forward-facing dashcam videos were recorded from test vehicles under real-world traffic conditions, capturing diverse interaction scenarios such as merges, cut-ins, and early lane changes. The finalized dataset comprises a total of 261 video clips. To ensure the robustness and diversity of the trajectory prediction under various driving maneuvers, these clips were categorized into 194 lane-keeping (LK) and 67 lane-changing (LC) scenarios. Each clip spans approximately 10 seconds at 30 frames per second (fps), yielding 300 frames of trajectory data per sequence. For model training and evaluation, we implemented an 80/20 split, resulting in a training set of 209 clips and a test set of 52 clips. All object trajectories were extracted through a unified detection and tracking pipeline and synchronized in a top-view coordinate space. The training samples were generated using a sliding window approach, utilizing 30 consecutive frames (1.0 second) as the observation period to predict the subsequent 150 frames.
